---
title: "Quarto Presentation Workflow"
subtitle: "presentation.qmd to presentation.pdf and presentation.html"
author: "Your Name"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, themes/thesis.scss]
    slide-number: c/t
    controls: true
    progress: true
    hash: true
    transition: fade
    footer: "presentation.qmd -> PDF / HTML"
    code-copy: true
    code-line-numbers: true
    highlight-style: github
  beamer:
    theme: metropolis
    colortheme: default
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    include-in-header:
      - assets/tex/presentation-preamble.tex
    aspectratio: 169
    navigation: empty
    colorlinks: true
---

# Main Workflow

## One Source, Two Outputs

The core workflow is:

```text
presentation.qmd
├── _output/presentation.pdf
└── _output/presentation.html
```

Render PDF slides:

```bash
quarto render presentation.qmd --to beamer
```

Render Reveal.js HTML:

```bash
quarto render presentation.qmd --to revealjs
```

## Toolchain

| Output | Quarto target | File | Required tools |
|---|---|---|---|
| PDF slides | `beamer` | `presentation.pdf` | Quarto, TinyTeX, XeLaTeX |
| HTML slides | `revealjs` | `presentation.html` | Quarto |
| SVG diagrams | referenced asset | `.svg` | PlantUML or Kroki |

## Beamer PDF Customization

The Beamer PDF loads `assets/tex/presentation-preamble.tex`.

That preamble adds:

- top section navigation bar
- Metropolis title accent line
- bottom three-part information bar
- ENS CJK font setup

## Columns

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="48%"}
### Source

```yaml
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, themes/thesis.scss]
  beamer:
    theme: metropolis
    include-in-header:
      - assets/tex/presentation-preamble.tex
```
:::

::: {.column width="48%"}
### Commands

```bash
quarto render presentation.qmd --to revealjs
quarto render presentation.qmd --to beamer
```
:::

::::

# Content Patterns

## Incremental List

::: incremental
- Install Quarto.
- Install TinyTeX for Beamer PDF output.
- Write `presentation.qmd`.
- Render explicit targets while learning.
:::

## Table

| Task | macOS / Linux | Windows PowerShell |
|---|---|---|
| Check Quarto | `quarto check` | `quarto check` |
| Render HTML | `quarto render presentation.qmd --to revealjs` | `quarto render presentation.qmd --to revealjs` |
| Render PDF | `quarto render presentation.qmd --to beamer` | `quarto render presentation.qmd --to beamer` |

## Figure from PlantUML

![Quarto rendering pipeline](diagrams/quarto-flow.svg){#fig-flow fig-alt="A flow diagram from QMD source through Quarto to HTML slides and PDF."}

Keep the editable source in `diagrams/quarto-flow.puml` and commit the rendered
`diagrams/quarto-flow.svg`.

## Callout

::: {.callout-note}
## PDF target name

For slide decks, use `--to beamer` to produce `presentation.pdf`. Use `--to pdf`
for article-style PDFs.
:::

## Quote

> A clean Quarto project should make the render target obvious from the source
> filename, the front matter, and the command line.

# Code and Math

## Code

```python
from pathlib import Path

source = Path("presentation.qmd")
print(source.with_suffix(".html"))
print(source.with_suffix(".pdf"))
```

## Math

$$
\text{output} =
\begin{cases}
\text{presentation.html}, & \text{target = revealjs} \\
\text{presentation.pdf}, & \text{target = beamer}
\end{cases}
$$

# Notes

## Speaker Notes

This slide has speaker notes in Reveal.js.

::: notes
Open speaker view in Reveal.js with the S key. Notes are useful for delivery
prompts that should not appear on the projected slide.
:::

## Checklist

- `presentation.qmd` exists at the repo root.
- `themes/thesis.scss` customizes Reveal.js.
- `diagrams/quarto-flow.svg` is generated from PlantUML.
- `_output/presentation.html` is the HTML deck.
- `_output/presentation.pdf` is the Beamer PDF deck.
